# coralogix_quota_rule (Resource)

Manages a single Coralogix quota policy. Provider credentials select the account and team context. Use exactly one of `log_rules` or `span_rules` to select the source type, and use `targets` with `dataset` and `dataspace` for target routing.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "coralogix_quota_rule" "logs_info" {
  name        = "Terraform example log quota rule"
  description = "Route info logs while quota policy is managed as code"
  enabled     = true
  priority    = "medium"

  application_rule = {
    rule_type = "starts_with"
    names     = ["prod"]
  }

  subsystem_rule = {
    rule_type = "is"
    names     = ["api"]
  }

  log_rules = {
    severities = ["info"]
  }
}

resource "coralogix_quota_rule" "dataset_target" {
  name     = "Terraform example target quota rule"
  enabled  = true

  log_rules = {
    dpxl_expression = "<v1> $d.severity == 'INFO'"
  }

  targets = [
    {
      dataspace = "default"
      dataset   = "logs"
      priority  = "medium"
      quota_based_priority_override = {
        usage_tiers = [
          {
            daily_quota_percentage = 50
            priority               = "medium"
          },
          {
            daily_quota_percentage = 80
            priority               = "low"
          },
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}

resource "coralogix_quota_rule" "span_rule" {
  name     = "Terraform example span quota rule"
  enabled  = true
  priority = "low"

  span_rules = {
    service_rule = {
      rule_type = "is"
      names     = ["checkout"]
    }

    action_rule = {
      rule_type = "includes"
      names     = ["POST"]
    }

    tag_rules = {
      "tags.http.status_code" = {
        rule_type = "starts_with"
        names     = ["5"]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Quota policy name.

### Optional

- `application_rule` (Attributes) Application names matched by this quota policy. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--application_rule))
- `archive_retention_id` (String) Archive retention ID applied by this quota policy.
- `description` (String) Quota policy description.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the quota policy is enabled.
- `log_rules` (Attributes) Log source-type matching rules. Exactly one of `log_rules` or `span_rules` must be set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--log_rules))
- `priority` (String) Policy-level quota priority. Required when `targets` is not configured. Do not set when target-level priority or priority overrides are configured. Valid values are ["block" "high" "low" "medium"].
- `quota_based_priority_override` (Attributes) Dynamically reassign the quota policy priority based on daily quota consumption tiers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--quota_based_priority_override))
- `span_rules` (Attributes) Span source-type matching rules. Exactly one of `log_rules` or `span_rules` must be set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--span_rules))
- `subsystem_rule` (Attributes) Subsystem names matched by this quota policy. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subsystem_rule))
- `targets` (Attributes List) Target routing destinations for this quota policy. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--targets))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Backend quota policy ID. Import accepts this value.
- `order` (Number) Backend evaluation order among quota policies.

<a id="nestedatt--application_rule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `application_rule`

Required:

- `names` (Set of String)

Optional:

- `rule_type` (String) Rule type. Valid values are ["includes" "is" "is_not" "starts_with" "unspecified"].


<a id="nestedatt--log_rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `log_rules`

Optional:

- `dpxl_expression` (String) DataPrime expression matched by this quota policy. Mutually exclusive with `severities`. The expression must include a version prefix, e.g. `<v1> $d.severity == 'INFO'`.
- `severities` (Set of String) Log severities matched by this quota policy. Valid values are ["critical" "debug" "error" "info" "verbose" "warning"]. Mutually exclusive with `dpxl_expression`.


<a id="nestedatt--quota_based_priority_override"></a>
### Nested Schema for `quota_based_priority_override`

Required:

- `usage_tiers` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--quota_based_priority_override--usage_tiers))

<a id="nestedatt--quota_based_priority_override--usage_tiers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `quota_based_priority_override.usage_tiers`

Required:

- `daily_quota_percentage` (Number) Daily quota consumption percentage at which this tier becomes active.
- `priority` (String) Priority to apply when this tier is active. Valid values are ["block" "high" "low" "medium"].



<a id="nestedatt--span_rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `span_rules`

Optional:

- `action_rule` (Attributes) Action names matched by this span quota policy. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--span_rules--action_rule))
- `service_rule` (Attributes) Service names matched by this span quota policy. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--span_rules--service_rule))
- `tag_rules` (Attributes Map) Span tag rules keyed by tag name, for example `tags.http.method`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--span_rules--tag_rules))

<a id="nestedatt--span_rules--action_rule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `span_rules.action_rule`

Required:

- `names` (Set of String)

Optional:

- `rule_type` (String) Rule type. Valid values are ["includes" "is" "is_not" "starts_with" "unspecified"].


<a id="nestedatt--span_rules--service_rule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `span_rules.service_rule`

Required:

- `names` (Set of String)

Optional:

- `rule_type` (String) Rule type. Valid values are ["includes" "is" "is_not" "starts_with" "unspecified"].


<a id="nestedatt--span_rules--tag_rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `span_rules.tag_rules`

Required:

- `names` (Set of String)

Optional:

- `rule_type` (String) Rule type. Valid values are ["includes" "is" "is_not" "starts_with" "unspecified"].



<a id="nestedatt--subsystem_rule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subsystem_rule`

Required:

- `names` (Set of String)

Optional:

- `rule_type` (String) Rule type. Valid values are ["includes" "is" "is_not" "starts_with" "unspecified"].


<a id="nestedatt--targets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `targets`

Required:

- `dataset` (String) Target dataset.

Optional:

- `archive_retention_id` (String) Archive retention ID applied to this target.
- `dataspace` (String) Target dataspace.
- `priority` (String) Target priority override. Valid values are ["block" "high" "low" "medium"].
- `quota_based_priority_override` (Attributes) Dynamically reassign this target priority based on daily quota consumption tiers. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--targets--quota_based_priority_override))

<a id="nestedatt--targets--quota_based_priority_override"></a>
### Nested Schema for `targets.quota_based_priority_override`

Required:

- `usage_tiers` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--targets--quota_based_priority_override--usage_tiers))

<a id="nestedatt--targets--quota_based_priority_override--usage_tiers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `targets.quota_based_priority_override.usage_tiers`

Required:

- `daily_quota_percentage` (Number) Daily quota consumption percentage at which this tier becomes active.
- `priority` (String) Priority to apply when this tier is active. Valid values are ["block" "high" "low" "medium"].
